message:
chore(beads): file 2 durability-audit beads [c5sgs, 39squ]

Follow-up sweep on j7hqe (closed): applied the same durability
pattern review to src/daemon/, src/indexer/, src/html_export/,
src/bookmarks.rs. daemon/html_export/bookmarks are clean;
src/indexer/mod.rs has two real gaps:

- c5sgs [MEDIUM, P2] — save_watch_state uses `fs::write` for the
  tmp file with no `sync_all` before the atomic rename. Classic
  "atomic rename without fsync of the tmp" pattern: the parent-
  dir fsync in `replace_file_from_temp` makes the dirent durable
  but not the file bytes. Crash window → watch_state.json
  resurfaces as a zero-length file on reboot and connectors lose
  their last_message_id_ms cursors.

- 39squ [MEDIUM, P2] — heartbeat_index_run_lock writes the lock
  file in-place via `fs::write` (O_TRUNC + write, no temp file,
  no fsync). Torn write during a heartbeat can leave partial
  contents that parse successfully but with wrong/missing fields,
  permanently poisoning the heartbeat and blocking another
  indexer from taking over a genuinely dead process.

Both can be fixed by routing through the existing correct helper
`write_json_pretty_atomically` (src/indexer/mod.rs:5188) or its
shape.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
